Phase 2c reference vector — t1_cache_derive_fingerprint.bin
============================================================

Spec attestation
----------------
T1 attests Rust `Cache::derive` byte-for-byte parity with the v2
RandomX fork's `randomx_init_cache` for the canonical seedhash. The
fingerprint covers both the 256 MiB Argon2d-derived `cache.memory`
and the 8 SuperscalarPrograms derived from the same seedhash via
Blake2Generator + `generateSuperscalar`. A divergence here
attributes a cache-derivation bug to (a) Argon2d output, (b)
SuperscalarHash program generation, or (c) the seedhash → blake2
generator wiring — by structure, isolatable from the Phase 2a / 2b
single-subsystem vectors that already pass.

Per RANDOMX_V2_PHASE2C_PLAN.md §5.7 (F7 T1).

Inputs (hardcoded in `_generator/phase2c/gen.cpp`)
--------------------------------------------------
seedhash  = [0x01, 0x02, 0x03, ..., 0x20]  — 32-byte canonical
                                              sequential test seed,
                                              shared with T2, T8
flag      = RANDOMX_FLAG_DEFAULT           — softAes (portability)

Output layout
-------------
Output = Blake2b-256 final hash over:
  - cache.memory                          (262144 × 1024 B = 256 MiB)
  - for each i in 0..8:
      - magic              "SSP1"         (4 B)
      - size_u16_le                        (2 B)
      - address_register_u8                (1 B)
      - reserved (0x00)                    (1 B)
      - size × {opcode u8, dst u8, src u8, mod u8, imm32_le}

Total bytes for this vector: 32.

Generator
---------
source      = ./_generator/phase2c/gen.cpp (mode t1)
build       = ./_generator/phase2c/Makefile (`make gen`)
command     = ./gen t1 > t1_cache_derive_fingerprint.bin
output_size = 32 bytes

Reference impl
--------------
fork        = external/randomx-v2/
pin         = aaafe71322df6602c21a5c72937ac284724ae561 (v2.0.1)
sources     = src/randomx.cpp (randomx_alloc_cache / randomx_init_cache),
              src/dataset.cpp (initCache),
              src/argon2_core.c + src/argon2_avx2.c + src/argon2_ssse3.c
                + src/argon2_ref.c,
              src/blake2/blake2b.c,
              src/blake2_generator.cpp,
              src/superscalar.cpp (generateSuperscalar)
function    = (composite — see "Output layout")

Notes
-----
The generator deliberately re-derives the 8 SuperscalarPrograms via
`Blake2Generator(key, keySize)` + `generateSuperscalar(prog, gen)`
directly, bypassing `cache->programs` (which `initCache` produces).
Reason: `dataset.cpp:131-138` post-processes `cache->programs`
after `generateSuperscalar` — for every `IMUL_RCP` instruction the
C reference REPLACES `imm32` in-place with `cache->reciprocalCache.size()`
(an index into the JIT-side reciprocal-cache table) and pushes the
precomputed `randomx_reciprocal(original_imm32)` value into that
side cache. The Rust port stores the ORIGINAL `imm32` and computes
the reciprocal on-the-fly in `execute_superscalar`'s `IMUL_RCP` arm
(no reciprocal side cache exists); the two storage shapes are
RESULT-equivalent (verified by T2 dataset-item parity) but
BYTE-divergent for serialization paths that consume `imm32`
directly. Hashing the pre-replacement programs gives the cache-
fingerprint vector that the Rust port matches by construction. See
`docs/design/RANDOMX_V2_PHASE2C_PLAN.md` §14 Round 0 R0-D11.

Phase
-----
Phase 2c (`docs/design/RANDOMX_V2_PHASE2C_PLAN.md` §5.7 / F7 T1,
§9 commit 7) — cache-derivation fingerprint. Consumed by Rust
test `t1_cache_derive_fingerprint_matches_fork_reference` in
`rust/shekyl-pow-randomx/src/cache.rs#mod tests`.
